programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: Part two of two. Although in disgrace, the amateur sleuth sets out to find the killer himself, but gets in a muddle with the FBI. Drama, starring Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: The murder of three prostitute leads the heavenly investigator to a trio of powerful businessmen. Drama, starring Tom Bosley as a Chicago parish priest

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A coin commemorating the rescue of a king's son from drowning is the clue to a murder

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny raise questions over the murder of a US Marine corporal found stabbed to death in Central Park. A case of mistaken identity arises as Stella, Lindsay and Dr Hawkes investigate a body in a burnt-out car, and Sheldon makes a surprising discovery after creating a facial reconstruction from the skull. With Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs revisits his home town to investigate an assault that left one marine dead and another badly injured, and enlists the help of his estranged father to identify the assailant. Suspicion initially falls on a local man whose daughter was dating the survivor of the attack - but later inquiries uncover a tangled tale of uncertain paternity and forbidden love. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Ralph Waite

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam, Callen, Kensi and Deeks work undercover on a case involving the deaths of multiple operatives killed during a raid on a drug lord's home. They are aware that inside information is being passed, and grow determined to flush out the mole responsible. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J, Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble flees to Mexico where he is compromised into assisting a local doctor in his search for a meningitis cure

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A Danish community and its proud furniture craftsmen are targets for the double-dealing of a sharp operator out for a quick profit

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Agent Callen poses as an arms trade contact in a bid to catch killer Michael Aaron Rivkin. Meanwhile, Tony fears Ziva may not be loyal to the team, and a psychologist uncovers the truth about the relationship between Gibbs and Agent Macy. With Chris O'Donnell, Louise Lombard and LL Cool J

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: When several high-school students are killed, Walker goes undercover as a teacher to investigate

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. The circumstances surrounding the man's demise lead the investigators to suspect someone was secretly trying to drive him insane, bringing them into contact with Samantha Ryan, the director of a division specialising in psychological warfare. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Kensi and Deeks investigate when a radical terrorist organisation notorious for orchestrating a series of bombings in the 1970s seems to have re-emerged. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah, Eric Christian Olsen and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A councillor's wife is found dead in suspicious circumstances and all the evidence points to her husband - but Steve believes the man is being framed by corrupt policemen. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A middle-aged man murders his teenage girlfriend to ensure she does not tell her family about their affair. Dr Sloan is not fooled by his alibi and sets out to uncover the truth. Guest starring Robert Stack (Airplane!)

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.desc: The murder of a port authority officer reveals Parsa's possible whereabouts as the team continues the hunt for the terrorist intent on wiping out NCIS. Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers faulty bulletproof vests in an abandoned truck filled with stolen USMC merchandise, and attempts to locate the source of the cargo and any vests that could have been sent to military personnel overseas. Crime drama, guest starring Challen Cates, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo confronts a man impersonating a Navy officer, which results in guns being drawn, and the only eyewitness to the case is the Special Agent's father - who is in town to disclose a secret to his son. The 250th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bomb detonates at a military charity concert, killing two men. Called to investigate, Tony and McGee try to determine whether the intended target was the 1980s rocker headlining the act or if the attack was part of a larger scheme against the military. Meanwhile, Jimmy prepares for parenthood. Keith Carradine guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gibbs joins forces with the FBI when the body of a congressman and former agent washes ashore in New Orleans. Together they try to determine if the murder could be linked to a well-known case the victim had previously worked on during his career. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. She identifies the murderer as Seal-turned-assassin Marcellus Dane, who is suspected of working for Casey Stratton - a member of black ops unit Phantom Eight. Guest starring Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An estate agent and two clients discover the body of Lt Com Maya Burris, a naval attache who had been working in Egypt, and her detective husband Nick arrives on the scene to demand answers. Using documents discovered on the victim's boat, Abby links her to Barry Norton, who is suspected of having sold government secrets to a Middle Eastern terrorist group known as the SRC

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs goes into his favourite diner for a cup of coffee and finds himself staring down the barrel of a gun, forcing him to question choices he has made in the past and present. The action then flashes back a few days to when he and the rest of the team members were investigating the death of a petty officer on board USS Gentry, which has been undergoing repairs in dry dock. The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy captain wearing a colourful costume underneath his uniform is shot and killed in a flower shop, and further investigation reveals he was leading a double life as a masked vigilante. Vance brings in Tony's ex-fiancee, journalist Wendy Miller, who claims the victim had been trying to expose a scandal, and as the team pursues the case, a real-estate scam is uncovered. Guest starring Perrey Reeves (Entourage)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An heiress is stabbed by a stalker, but the most likely suspect is found dead - and the case is complicated when an X-ray of the victim reveals surprising results. Medical whodunit, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan is forced to step in and defend Jesse against accusations that he accidentally caused the death of a basketball player. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the case of an actress who died of an infection seemingly contracted during routine cosmetic surgery at a clinic. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A special agent forms a team of geniuses to combat hi-tech threats of the modern age, and hires former child prodigy Walter O'Brien to lead the gang. Their first mission sees them assigned by the government to help land planes at Los Angeles airport after the communications system shuts down due to a bug. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The governor's daughter is 'bio-hacked' and given a deadly virus. She only has 24 hours to live, so Scorpion is called in to save her. However, the mission causes tension with Walter after he receives a letter detailing his sister Megan's worsening multiple sclerosis. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs discovers a link between the murder of a petty officer and the break-up of his team, leading him and new director Leon Vance to uncover a case of blackmail involving an NCIS agent. The duo single out three suspects, but learn vital information has been stolen, and realise the whole Persian Gulf Fleet could be in danger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A lieutenant apparently commits suicide by jumping from an aircraft carrier and Ziva and McGee are dispatched to inform the widow - but arrive to find that she has been killed. Subsequent inquiries reveal that the injuries on her body were inflicted after death and the murder scene was carefully staged to mislead investigators. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs receives a call from his old friend Senator Patrick Kiley, who claims he is being framed for the murder of a naval commander, and seeks help to clear his name. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

Drama, starring John Michie, Blythe Duff and Hugo Speer

programme.desc: Part two of two. Evidence reveals Mason Kemble was present at his classmate's murder and Neil arrests him. programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary's old school friend Dorothy invites the duo to restore the gardens of her beautiful villa on the French Riviera so the property can be opened to the public. The plan arouses bitter hostility among the neighbours and long-held family secrets start to surface, leading to murder. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to investigate why newly planted roses at an upmarket preparatory school keep dying. After witnessing the arcane ritual of the Night of the Stag, they detect tensions between modernising headmaster Quentin Marshall and established members of his staff who are harbouring secrets someone is prepared to kill for. Drama, guest starring Anthony Andrews (Brideshead Revisited), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered sleuths are hired to work behind the scenes of a garden makeover TV show, but Rosemary has an accident while standing in for the programme's star and ends up in hospital. Realising someone is trying to sabotage the series, Laura teams up with no-nonsense presenter Quinnie Dorell (Belinda Lang) to catch the culprit. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: As the Brokenwood Golf Club gathers for its Wednesday morning round, a zombie-like figure lurches from the morning mist identified as Alison Stone, the wealthy club owner. Her face red and blistering, she collapses into a bunker and dies leaving Mike and Kristin to investigate an apparent homicide by poisoning. Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu must clear their names when they are framed for various crimes by some mysterious doppelgangers. Starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: A past case returns to haunt Rebus when he is called to a local landmark, where the remains of a prostitute have been found. The inspector is convinced a member of the Scottish Parliament is responsible - but his efforts to secure a conviction are hampered by the arrival of a high-flying colleague drafted in by his superior. Crime drama, starring Ken Stott

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective is called to a remote farmhouse, where he finds an elderly man has been murdered, and his wife is close to death. He hears the woman mutter something before dying, but cannot decipher her words. When he is pressured into revealing what he believes they were, the consequences of his reply have a serious impact on life in the town of Ystad. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh, Sarah Smart and Richard McCabe, with Dearbhla Molloy and June Watson

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's knight in shining armour arrives in the form of paroled rock legend Abel Kane, whisking them off on a winter break to Majorca, where yet more unexpected good fortune awaits them. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam suspects a riot is in the offing when a football fan is killed. Knowing the problem will escalate as time goes by, the detective becomes determined to nip the violence in the bud, so he, Gene and Annie go undercover in a pub to catch the culprits. However, he realises the derby match that will be the focus of the conflict is the last one he went to with his dad when he was four years old

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: On her first day as an Inspector, Rachel co-ordinates a joint raid with CID on a crack den, only to find the main culprits have been tipped off in advance. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Rachel is frustrated when two brothers both offer conflicting versions of the stabbing of a drug dealer and Leon blots his copybook with his boss once more through his determination to help. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A hysterical mother reports her eight-year-old daughter missing and the child's father immediately falls under suspicion - until a classmate tells the police his father was parked outside the school at the time of the disappearance. When a body is finally found there is not enough evidence to hold either suspect, but a shocking third possibility begins to emerge

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. Nurse Crane offers what support she can - but the girl's life is endangered when the young lovers go on the run. A case of petty theft brings a group of Irish travellers to the attention of the midwives, who end up treating a newborn infant and looking after an expectant mother, and it seems Fred's romance is on the up when he asks Violet to accompany him to a fundraising dance

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A water shortage means that the maternity home is busier than ever and two old schoolmates are reunited, but Marion shuns Shirley's attempts at renewing their friendship until they are brought together later in an unexpected way. Elsewhere, Barbara makes a revolutionary suggestion to help Sister Mary Cynthia treat the bedsores of her patient Tommy Mills, whose wife Gert may be more in need of care than he is. Fred thinks about taking the next step in his relationship with Violet. Drama, guest starring Una Stubbs and David Ryall

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie and Sister Mary Cynthia team up to support a deaf mother-to-be, while another patient causes great concern when a case of morning sickness proves more serious than initially suspected. On the personal front, handyman Fred's daughter is less than delighted to hear about his forthcoming wedding. Will he be forced to choose between her and Violet?

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Moth
programme.desc: Feature-length version of the drama set at the turn of the 20th century, about the fortunes of a carpenter who moves to the country to start work in his uncle's furniture business, but finds himself forced to seek employment with a beguiling woman known locally as the Moth. Jack Davenport and Justine Waddell star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A new midwife joins the team as the area is rocked by power cuts and blizzards. The new arrival is delayed by the weather and falls ill as soon as she arrives. However, she is quickly dragged from her sick bed to assist with a difficult birth. Nurse Crane and Dr Turner treat an elderly cancer patient who faces losing her house due to slum clearances, and try to convince Sergeant Woolf to allow her to die at home

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth receives a cryptic letter from a recently deceased acquaintance asking her to conduct a mysterious investigation. Instructed only to go on a coach tour of historic homes around England, she uncovers murder and intrigue. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, with Peter Tilbury, John Horsley and Helen Cherry

programme.title: The Secret Diaries of Miss Anne Lister
programme.desc: Drama, starring Maxine Peake as 19th-century Yorkshire landowner, industrialist and traveller Anne Lister, who defied the conventions of her times by living with her female lover. She kept an account of her emotions in a journal, a sizeable portion of which was written in code, and the recent deciphering of this diary offers an insight into the life of the woman who has been called Britain's first modern lesbian. With Susan Lynch, Christine Bottomley, Anna Madeley, Dean Lennox Kelly and Tina O'Brien

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' daring and controversial adaptation of Sarah Waters' novel of lesbian love set in the 1890s. The three-part drama begins as Nan Astley realises she is attracted to other women and embarks on a series of adventures, which take her from her parents' oyster parlour in Whitstable to the glamour of London. Starring Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Realising she cannot return to her family life in Whitstable, Nan attempts to earn money by posing as a male prostitute on the streets of London, disguising herself with the aid of stage costumes. It isn't long before she meets a wealthy society widow who offers her a better life - at a perilous price. Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor star

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Part three of three. Thrown out by Diana and left destitute by Blake, Nan turns to Florence for help. programme.title: Three Days That Shook Football
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes of a chaotic three days in European football, as a proposed Super League was announced and then collapsed when faced with a wall of opposition

programme.title: The Hurt Locker (2008)
programme.desc: Two American bomb disposal experts operating in Iraq are assigned a new leader after their previous one dies in action. However, the sergeant's reckless attitude and love of battle consistently puts them at risk. Kathryn Bigelow's Oscar-winning drama, starring Jeremy Renner, Anthony Mackie, Guy Pearce and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Pilgrims Les Dennis, Lesley Joseph, Brendan Cole, Stephen K Amos, Katy Brand, Greg Rutherford, Dana and Mehreen Baig visit Bagno Vignoni - well known for its hot springs - where Greg and Brendan take the plunge in the public pool. Meanwhile, at the top of the hillside, Dana, Stephen, Les and Katy visit the Roman bath formerly used by Popes, and explore the history of St Catherine of Siena, who also used to stay here. The group members travel on to the medieval city of Viterbo, from where they will have to walk every step of the final 100km to Rome. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, where the red and blue teams explore an antique centre in the company of experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian. The auction of their gatherings is later held in nearby Chiswick

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits Salisbury Cathedral to celebrate 30 years of the girls' choir, and Laura Wright meets Christian musicians at Coventry's Nexus Institute of Creative Arts

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a mother and daughter find a country home in Norfolk with a budget of £335,000, and follows a paper trail to uncover an endangered decorative heritage craft

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of British and international TV programmes and talent, with the programme coming from the BBC's Television Centre in White City, west London. The Crown, I Hate Suzie, Gangs of London and Save Me Too are up for Best Drama Series, while other nominees include John Boyega, Josh O'Connor, Daisy Edgar-Jones, Graham Norton and Ant & Dec and the show features performances by Years & Years and Alexis Ffrench

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Prison drama, starring Sean Bean and Stephen Graham. A newly arrived prisoner has to adapt quickly to his dangerous new surroundings, while reflecting on the crime that brought him here. He finds himself sharing a cell with a troubled young man who murdered his father and is suffering panic attacks. Meanwhile, an officer encounters a threat against his son, who is serving time in a different prison

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from recent series, including Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness attempting to drive Insurance write offs around a terrifying Wall of Death. Plus, Chris reviews the Ferrari SF90 and the boys race around Alton Towers in electric cars

programme.title: Wild Weekends
programme.desc: BBC Radio 1 DJ Jeremiah Asiamah pushes three complete strangers outside of their comfort zones on a voyage of self-discovery in Torridon in the Highlands. Jeremiah's friend and outdoorsman Zeki Basan shows them how to set up camp under the stars, offers alternative cooking methods and helps them conquer an epic climb of more than 2,000ft

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A mother and daughter from Buckinghamshire answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win a life-changing sum they plan to put towards their love of animals. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Adrian and Gail dream of a contemporary garden to match their house in Holmfirth, but it must have an Australian twist. Charlie and the Rich Brothers try to achieve it for £5,000

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts finds a surprise hidden in a bungalow in Stafford and Dion Dublin heads to a semi-detached in Stoke-on-Trent. Plus, revisiting a former church in West Yorkshire, whose newlywed owners planned to transformed it into their dream forever home

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedian Rachel Parris, chef Rick Stein, actor and comedian Rob Delaney and presenter Vick Hope. They share favourite books as well as reviewing the week's two club picks - The Lying Life of Adults by Elena Ferrante, and Should We Fall Behind by Sharon Duggal

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment. In Basingstoke he visits a glorious neo-Gothic stately home set in the North Wessex Downs

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Shropshire with Sandra Zhang from Coventry and Ian Tully from Preston. They will be spending their hard earned cash in the hope they can make a profit

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Tommy Banks, Josh Overington and Danny Parker continue the battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to the North East final

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest Judy Murray - mother of British number one tennis player Andy - as the programme continues its Wimbledon-themed series

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Featuring an island paradise for puffins in Scotland and golden hares in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Chris Packham checks in on the live nests, and Michaela Strachan reports on the plight of the Norfolk plovers

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team focus on the array of weird and wonderful creatures on the UK's seashore, and Richard Taylor Jones goes in search of the barrel jellyfish

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell hunt for objects to take to auction at Blackburn Cathedral, Lancashire. Charles values a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip's eye. Presenter Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection - local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Clara Amfo, Anneka Rice and Al Murray compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is at Elgin's port, the coastal town of Lossiemouth, where the Labour Party's first prime minister James Ramsay MacDonald was born. Nearby, Michael finds a remote boarding school established in the 1930s and famous today both for its unusual ideas and its royal former pupils. His penultimate stop is Inverness, where he uncovers the work of female photographer MEM Donaldson, who documented a Highland way of life that was rapidly disappearing. Michael's journey ends at Loch Ness, where he joins a Deep Scan research team scouring the deep for signs of the elusive monster

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day three of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. The last Test series between the sides came in the winter of 2019, when New Zealand recorded a 1-0 series victory thanks to a comprehensive victory at Bay Oval and a draw at Seddon Park, but Joe Root's men will have been hopeful of making home advantage count on this occasion

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: There's theft in the Scottish seas - but do great skuas deserve their reputation as pirates of the skies? Megan McCubbin goes to the Welsh border to investigate why restoring peatland bog is so important for wildlife. There's also a live round-up of the wild soap operas playing out in Norfolk, and a peek at a secretive owl in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Frances Tophill introduce a special Gardeners' World from Wakehurst, Kew's wild botanic garden in Sussex. They not only explore the gardens but also meet the gardeners, joining in on their planting projects and finding out more about the UK's largest conservation project, the Millennium Seed Bank

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Joan Armatrading chats to Jools and picks her favourite clips from the Later archive, including appearances by Tinie Tempah, the Ting Tings and Skepta. Rock band Field Music perform No Pressure at the Empire Theatre in their native Sunderland, and there is film of Los Angeles-based group Gabriels playing the title track from their debut EP Love and Hate in a Different Time

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories, with Mark Urban

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Anders undergoes brain surgery and his memory begins to return - revealing what happened to the 13th colony, how the five remaining Cylons survived and how their humanoid counterparts gained the ability to resurrect themselves. Meanwhile, it is discovered the Galactica's much-needed repairs require the use of Cylon technology - risking a rebellion if the decision were made to proceed

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Ellen Tigh returns to the Galactica, but her passionate reunion with Saul is short-lived when she learns of his relationship with Six and their expected child. The remaining Cylons are delighted by her return, however, and she begins to encourage them to leave and begin a new life away from the ship

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Identical twin brothers George and Peter who fled Nazi Germany in 1939 search for the family of the man who helped them escape. Plus, Clare, who housed 21 child refugees from wartorn Bosnia, looks for their mystery schoolgirl translator Petra, whose language skills allowed the children to properly communicate and share the horrors they had fled. Presented by Anita Rani

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out a super-powered squirrel that can withstand the freezing temperatures of the Arctic winter

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team creates a den for 11-year-old Yusuf, hopes to use his love of computer coding to become an army intelligence officer

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield hosts as team captains Harry and Ria battle it out, and are joined by CBBC's Rhys Stephenson and comedians Lou Conran, Sam Fletcher and Bec Hill

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: With a performance by rising star Gracey

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve focuses on the biggest dinosaurs to have ever walked the earth, beginning with the Argentinosaurus

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: A trip through the natural history archives with some of the BBC's favourite wildlife presenters as they share some of their most memorable adventures across Asia. Steve Backshall looks back on one particularly unnerving adventure in Indonesia when he and his crew were chased by a group of Komodo dragons, while Chris Packham recalls how he took photographs of the Orang Rimba people, hunter-gatherers who live in the Sumatran rainforest

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: There's theft in the Scottish seas - but do great skuas deserve their reputation as pirates of the skies? Megan McCubbin goes to the Welsh border to investigate why restoring peatland bog is so important for wildlife. There's also a live round-up of the wild soap operas playing out in Norfolk, and a peek at a secretive owl in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: York City Knights v St Helens (Kick-off 11.15am). Tanya Arnold presents coverage of the final from Leigh Sports Village. With commentary from Sharon Shortle

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers use their smartphones to capture fast-moving action in a boxing arena, and are set further challenges by artist Maryam Wahid and celebrity stylist Cheryl Konteh

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist reflects on the most challenging and intriguing questions facing science today, using material from his past programmes and the latest scientific research. He begins by looking at how far the exploration of the cosmos can go, believing that humanity is at the start of a new age of travel where space flight is on the verge of becoming routine

programme.title: D-Day: The Last Heroes
programme.desc: The concluding part of historian Dan Snow's documentary tells the powerful and heroic stories of those who risked their lives on the beaches of Normandy to save the world

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg looks back at the Second World War by visiting Osterley Park in Middlesex, site of the first Home Guard training school. Robert Brown, from North Yorkshire, encounters his father as a company commander, and Dad's Army co-writer Jimmy Perry explains how his experience in the Home Guard inspired him to create the hit comedy

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers or Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the concluding semi-final from Leigh Sports Village, as the line-up for July's final at Wembley Stadium is confirmed. The Tigers have not lifted this trophy since 1986, while the Wolves have triumphed in this competition on four occasions since 2009, with the most recent of those victories coming two years ago. With commentary from Matt Newsum

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day four of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. The sides last met in this format on English soil in 2015, when the hosts recorded a 124-run victory here, before the Black Caps ensured the series ended in a draw by prevailing by 199 runs at Headingley

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's last full year as British prime minister contrasted with global political change, while in the music world Pete Waterman's final year of chart domination heralded the dawn of Madchester. Some female artists brought attitude and power to 1989, while an unlikely duet between a 1960s legend and an 1980s icon proved a chart-topper and Jive Bunny kept the less discerning happy. As far as Top of the Pops was concerned, Radio 1's old guard was stood down as a team of fresh-faced recruits helmed the show. Contributors include Jason Donovan, Lisa Stansfield, Shaun Ryder, Chris Rea, Marc Almond, Sharleen Spiteri, Jazzie B and Jenny Powell

programme.title: Tina Turner at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of the singer's performances on BBC programmes over the years, featuring some of her biggest hits from the 1970s, '80s and '90s

programme.title: Tina Turner: Simply the Best
programme.desc: The singer chats to BBC arts editor Will Gompertz about the highs and lows of her career and bring her well-documented life story up to date. In a wide-ranging conversation, she talks about the tragedy of her eldest son's recent suicide, how an unexpected stroke affected the early days of her second marriage, and how her life was saved when her husband became her kidney donor. She also discusses the musical collaborations that saw her become one of the icons of the 1980s, and the abusive relationship with ex-husband Ike Turner, which set her on the road to stardom, but also nearly destroyed her

programme.title: Tina Live!
programme.desc: A 2009 concert by Tina Turner in the Dutch city of Arnhem as part of her 50th anniversary tour, which saw her perform 90 shows in more than 40 different cities across North America and Europe during 2008 and 2009. The set contains some of Tina's best-loved songs, including The Best, What's Love Got to Do With It and Proud Mary

programme.title: Three Summers (2017)
programme.desc: Over the course of three years, two musicians meet and fall in love at a music festival in Western Australia amidst a microcosm of the country's society. Romantic comedy, written and directed by Ben Elton and starring Robert Sheehan, Rebecca Breeds and Michael Caton

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler demonstrates how to grow herbs and flowers and use them as ingredients in a selection of dishes, including herb omelettes, bread with rosemary and salads. She also reveals how they are important to attract wildlife to gardens, something essential to the health and productivity of any allotment

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: In Stechford, Birmingham, Chris Bavin meets a group of determined volunteers entering their town into 'It's Your Neighbourhood', the entry level scheme of Bloom

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Frances Tophill introduce a special Gardeners' World from Wakehurst, Kew's wild botanic garden in West Sussex. They not only explore the gardens but also meet the gardeners, joining in on their planting projects and finding out more about the UK's largest conservation project, the Millennium Seed Bank

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales to report on plans for a major energy project harnessing the power of the tide, discovering what local people make of the plan and assess its potential impact on wildlife and fishing. Matt also checks on the health of a critical crop of carbon-capturing sea grass, and helps launch new research into mountain goats, while Joe Crowley discovers why angel sharks are being sighted off the Welsh coast more frequently

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: In a special episode, Dougie and team meet the young people who will steer the future of our countryside - the young farmers, crofters, conservationists and rural craftspeople. Anne visits a young couple who have just taken on a farm in Galloway, Dougie is in the north-west with a dynamic woman who won Young Crofter of the Year and Euan finds out how young folk can be encouraged to stay in the countryside

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole and Mairi prove that they are some of the best bargain hunters, as they create a new bargain border in Beechgrove for the price of a little work. It's an allotment special this week. and Calum Clunie is in his Leven allotment, where he is growing for upcoming shows and this week, he proves that he really knows his oversized onions

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer turns the contents of his weekly shop into seven dishes - one for each day of the week - adding new flavours to everyday recipes and creating culinary magic in just one pan. He begins by showing how to make the most of an abundance of ingredients, whether a special offer at the shops or extra produce from the garden

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Will Foster and Lynsey Elliott compete to remodel a home in Gosport, Hampshire, that holds 20 years of family memories and a lifetime of antiques and heirlooms for soon-to-be grandparents Lisa and Judy. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Live Triathlon
programme.desc: Coverage of the second leg of the Triathlon World Championship Series in Leeds. With the Olympics little more than a month away, the elite races in West Yorkshire represent one of the last chances for athletes to book their place in Tokyo - they include double Olympic champion Alistair Brownlee, who is returning from injury, as well as rising star Alex Yee. The women's race will also see a strong British team, including previous Leeds winners, Olympic bronze medallist Vicky Holland and current world champion, Georgia Taylor-Brown, both of whom have already made the British team for Tokyo. Also competing will be Scotland's Beth Potter, who recently ran the second-fastest road 5km ever for woman

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day five of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. This is the 18th overall Test match between these nations to be staged at 'the home of cricket', and if the black Caps can claim a victory it will be just the second they have recorded

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters reflect on memorable trips to the rainforest, with Chris Packham recalling a visit to Pipeline Road in Panama when he saw a staggering array of birds. Steve Backshall recounts his climb of a sheer sandstone mountain in Venezuela, while Gordon Buchanan talks about his four years living in the rainforest while learning his trade as a cameraman

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist looks at attempts to answer one of the most profound questions we can ask - are we alone in the universe? With scientists sending space probes to the furthest reaches of our solar system and beyond, the scientific search for alien life has begun and Brian explains why this quest deserves to be taken seriously, and explores the chances of extraterrestrials being found

programme.title: Arctic with Bruce Parry
programme.desc: The explorer travels to Qaanaaq, an Inuit town in the far north of Greenland, where he learns some of the skills that have enabled the native people to survive amid the harsh wilderness, including dog-sled driving and extracting water from sea ice. Hunting also remains a key part of life for the community, with supermarket prices for some staple foods set at 50 per cent more than in mainland Europe - but Bruce discovers the impact of global warming could lead to this traditional way of life dying out entirely

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin tries to help Jerome see sense and stop him taking revenge on Manboy - which would jeopardise his own, much more subtle plan for retribution

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Despite Franklin's best efforts to hold it back, the tide of change has finally come to his family, but Teddy seems to be offering a way out. Gustavo goes in search of a face from his past

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian convinces Andrei to return to hospital to have the surgery he needs, so Fletch warns Lucky to call the social services. A nervous Max needs all the support she can get as the day of her operation arrives, and Ange and Josh risk being found out when Dom and Chloe catch them sneaking around together

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pop Go the Sixties
programme.desc: Procol Harum's 1967 Top of the Pops performance of A Whiter Shade of Pale

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: An hour-long compilation of classic singles highlighting the growing popularity of the singer-songwriter genre during the 1970s, which became one of the decade's defining styles. Featuring music by Elton John, James Taylor, Cat Stevens, Harry Nilsson, Neil Diamond, Steve Goodman, Joni Mitchell and Neil Young

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 31 January 1991 and featuring EMF, Nomad and Soho

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 7 February 1991 and featuring the KLF, Kenny Thomas and Kim Appleby

programme.title: Once Were Brothers: Robbie Robertson and the Band (2019)
programme.desc: The story of how the Canadian guitarist overcame adversity and found camaraderie alongside the four other men who would become his brothers in music in the Band. The film blends rare archival footage, photography, songs and interviews with Robertson's friends and collaborators, including Martin Scorsese, Bruce Springsteen, Eric Clapton and Van Morrison

programme.title: Guitar, Drum and Bass
programme.desc: The American guitarist recounts the story of the quest for new guitar sounds, which has driven pop music from Les Paul's first guitar to Bo Diddley's tremolo, Duane Eddy's whammy bar, Keith Richards' fuzz pedal, the Who's feedback, the Byrds' 12 string, Hendrix's wah wah pedal, Uli Roth and Van Halen's shredding, the Edge's digital delay, Ry Cooder's slide and KT Tunstall and Ed Sheeran's looper pedals. Contributors include Duane Eddy, Roger McGuinn, the Edge, Bonnie Raitt, Seasick Steve, KT Tunstall, Joe Bonamassa, Uli Jon Roth, Vernon Reid, Heart's Nancy Wilson, the Runaways' Lita Ford and producer Shel Talmy

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: The 6Music presenter embarks on a quest to understand Latin Music's enduring appeal. Arriving in Rio, Huey learns that Brazil's newly elected government has come out against the hedonistic atmosphere of carnival and that Mangueira Samba School group is just one of many planning a protest. Later, he meets Gilberto Gil, who pioneered a new, politically conscious sound known as Tropicália. It was so radical - and its social implications so profound - that Gil was imprisoned and finally exiled in 1969. In Salvador, Huey meets percussionist Carlinhos Brown to talk about how afro positive music has shaped the sound of Brazil

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: Bossacucanova is a Brazilian musical group combining traditional Bossa Nova with electronica. Pioneer of bossa nova Roberto Menescal, joins the band - which co-comprises his son Márcio - in their Rio studio, for a re-interpreting of classic Bossa hits. With contributions by Mustafa Baba Aussa and Marcio Menescal. Partly in Portuguese with English subtitles

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 31 January 1991 and featuring EMF, Nomad and Soho

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 7 February 1991 and featuring the KLF, Kenny Thomas and Kim Appleby

programme.title: Once Were Brothers: Robbie Robertson and the Band (2019)
programme.desc: The story of how the Canadian guitarist overcame adversity and found camaraderie alongside the four other men who would become his brothers in music in the Band. The film blends rare archival footage, photography, songs and interviews with Robertson's friends and collaborators, including Martin Scorsese, Bruce Springsteen, Eric Clapton and Van Morrison

programme.title: How the Celts Saved Britain
programme.desc: Conclusion. Dan Snow examines the ways in which the Celts saved Britain from cultural oblivion during the Dark Ages. He follows in the footsteps of Ireland's earliest missionaries as they ventured through treacherous barbarian territory, bringing literacy and technology to the future nations of Scotland and England

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The intrepid traveller continues his journey around eastern Europe by visiting the Baltic states of Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania. He tells the story of how three tiny nations escaped the oppression of the Soviet Union and reclaimed a culture that was once thought lost. Michael celebrates the pagan festival of Jaani, embarks on a session of fire-walking, and comes to understand why Lithuania is often referred to as 'A Singing Nation'

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: Salvo requests a transfer to Genoa to be closer to Livia, but it is clear from his demeanour he is reluctant to leave his beloved Sicily. However, all thoughts of relocating are put from his mind when a Milanese barmaid living in Vigata disappears. Rumours abound about her excessive promiscuity, and people begin to jump to sordid conclusions. Montalbano balances this case with another involving a spate of unusual burglaries, in which the victims report the theft of bafflingly small amounts of money. Crime drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victim's skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund and Meyer become convinced there is a link between Nanna's murder and a case that has remained unsolved for 15 years, and frantically try to find evidence. Meanwhile, Pernille and Theis are confused by the fact the case is far from closed and Hartmann puts everything on the line in a bid to win the election. Starring Sofie Grabol and Soren Malling. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund and Meyer get more than they bargained for when they visit a warehouse to search for evidence, while a prime suspect disappears. Hartmann struggles to get back into the political arena - but is contacted by a journalist whose efforts to dig up dirt have had some success. Theis and Pernille take their sons to see the new house. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund finds the odds stacked against her as the search for the missing suspect takes an unexpected turn. Meanwhile, Hartmann and Bremer take part in a dramatic live TV debate amid a stream of revelations. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund is determined to catch Nanna's killer despite being taken off the case, and Hartmann is on a downward spiral - until he receives crucial information. Meanwhile, Pernille and Theis invite friends and family to Anton's birthday party - but a sinister twist to the evening is in store. In Danish

programme.title: The Turn of the Screw
programme.desc: OperaGlass Works' stage production of Britten's The Turn of the Screw, filmed on location at Wilton's Music Hall. The production was in rehearsals when the spring lockdown was declared in 2020, so the show has been reinvented as a hybrid of film-making and live theatre capture. Directed by Dominic Best, Selina Cadell and Eliza Thompson, and starring Robert Murray and Rhian Lois

programme.title: Benjamin Britten on Camera
programme.desc: Britten was one of the great classical composers of the broadcasting age and his music was regularly performed on radio and TV throughout his working life. This documentary outlines his broadcasting legacy by meeting some of the musicians he wrote for, visiting the Suffolk coast that inspired so much of his work, and hearing from the producers who brought his music to millions. With contributions by David Attenborough, Michael Crawford, Humphrey Burton and soprano April Cantelo. Narrated by James Naughtie

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the art, music and culture of Senegal, discovering a country with a cultural influence far beyond its size, with dynamic film, fashion and hip-hop scenes

programme.title: Searching for Shergar
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's most valuable racehorses, Shergar, which was famously abducted from its stables in 1983, at the height of the Troubles, and never seen again, with no ransom being paid and the kidnappers having never been brought to justice. Thirty seven years on, Alison Millar sets out to see if changed times will help her unearth the mystery once and for all - including trying to locate the place where the horse was buried, in the countryside around the village of Ballinamore

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of the planet's largest marine mammals and the secrets of their survival. Cameramen Doug Allan and Didier Noirot travel from the Indian Ocean to the Arctic seas, filming 200-ton blue whales and fighting humpbacks. The team also discovers that southern right whales possess a pair of one-ton testicles and reveals how Arctic bowheads can live to be more than 200 years old. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Documentary following Dougie Wallace as he finishes an acclaimed photography series documenting the super-rich some of London's most wealthy and exclusive postcodes. The film offers a personal and provocative portrait of one of the world's leading street photographers, as he captures life on the streets of places such as Knightsbridge and Chelsea

programme.title: Benjamin Britten on Camera
programme.desc: Britten was one of the great classical composers of the broadcasting age and his music was regularly performed on radio and TV throughout his working life. This documentary outlines his broadcasting legacy by meeting some of the musicians he wrote for, visiting the Suffolk coast that inspired so much of his work, and hearing from the producers who brought his music to millions. With contributions by David Attenborough, Michael Crawford, Humphrey Burton and soprano April Cantelo. Narrated by James Naughtie

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the deadly impact of an incendiary bomb that set fire to St Peter's Church in the historic heart of Bristol on the night of Sunday November 24, 1940. Almost 80 years later, Geoffrey Serle, who had been attending church with his father when the raid began, recounts their desperate attempt to find shelter amongst the chaos. The film also uses accounts from the time, recalling how Bristol's firefighters simply could not cope

Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Nigel’s bag are a toy pig, a toy sheep and a toy chicken, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around things you might find at a farm. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate farm objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! programme.desc: Join Maddie as she discovers that astronauts live in space on a spacecraft called the International Space Station and that there have been different astronauts living there for over 20 years. Maddie shows us how astronauts live and work on the International Space Station and do lots of different jobs and research in order to learn more about space and our planet Earth. We explore a laboratory where they do experiments, the food they eat, how they wash and even how they go to the toilet. We find out that the space station is made from thousands of parts that were put together by astronauts in space, and we learn that it’s also a home because it’s a place where astronauts eat, sleep and have fun.

programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. tries to keep up with a family of Huggy Rollers as they roll along together. B.O.T. ends up encased in a snowball, but the snow melts away when the Rollers give it a big warm hug!

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how the robots astronauts use in space have helped us to create robots we use here on Earth. We discover there are lots of different types of space robots, robonauts that help astronauts to do jobs, space probes that travel to far off parts of the solar system, and landers and rovers - robots that land on planets like Mars to collect information. We learn how robots are sent on special missions to Mars and how they take pictures and send information back to Earth. Maddie visits a research centre to meet a Mars rover robot and uses her special cameras to help us see how it moves over the ground.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The sheep and his friends discover their playground has been covered by a layer of frost, but while everyone else has fun sliding around, Kid struggles to keep his balance

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey can’t agree on one simple question: Can Grannies do the floss dance?

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: When Bella is elected class council representative, Sam is a little put out. She has lots of ideas to help make school better and fancied having the role for herself. Bella then ignores all the suggestions from her classmates and consequently upsets Mali too. Zainab, Kobi and David despair when Mali, Sam and Bella end up ignoring each other. They must show the three friends that the only way forward is to talk to each other

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: With the twins due to start school, two of their teachers come for a home visit. Topsy cannot wait to show Miss Terry all of her toys, but Tim is shy

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the day after the Brooklyn-Bells' wedding and time to say goodbye to the puppy

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: It’s a big day in the garden as the residents prepare for the Potting Shed Amateur Dramatics Society’s annual show. Betty the Mouse is practicing her singing and Sid the Snail is getting ready for a rap, but Fred the Pill Millipede is struggling with his routine. He loves to tap dance, but it’s not easy when you have as many legs as he does. The other performers have lots of advice for Fred, but finding confidence under pressure can be a real challenge! Can he overcome his nerves in time for the big show?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: When Henry and his family go on a trip to a nearby pond, it’s fishing nets at the ready as they try their hand at a spot of shrimping! It’s not easy as the tiny shrimp are good at hiding. Luckily, Henry comes up with a plan – but will it work? And will they catch any shrimp? Later, it’s Henry who is good at hiding in a family game of hide-and-seek. But will his clever hiding place be discovered?

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Oliver, Reau, Minnie, Theo, Francis and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has a toy umbrella inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different weather-themed toys or objects too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate weather-themed toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show how they’ve been discovering the weather too. The nursery rhyme I See Sunshine features, as does a story about Baby Bear cloud spotting. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Bobbles dresses as Beowulf and meets some beastly creatures at the bottom of the sea, while Simon and Donna go deep beneath the waves

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: It’s lunchtime on Bluey's back deck! But every BBQ needs a side salad, so Bingo’s relaxing sit-down will have to wait until she finds every colour of pepper her cousins want!

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour purple, from lavender to purple sea urchins. We explore purple found in nature as well as the association of the colour to royalty. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour purple.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie finds out how cable cars work and visits a viewing tower to see how it is made

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran go to the Library and JoJo gets her first ever library card!

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The feisty seven-year-old and her classmates learn about the legend of the singing seals from Mr Mackie during a lesson on the beach. However, she cannot hear the creatures, and spends the next few days doing her upmost to get them to sing for her

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: A ballad about the importance of family and friends

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Tortoise embarks a mission through Sarah and Duck's house, so the pair try to work out what he is crawling toward

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: It is all work and no play for Lola - until Marv comes round for a game of cards

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: When Mo wakes up with a cold, who s there to take over mum duties? Tee of course!

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major Clanger makes a new garden trolley for Mother

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines go to meet the Wottingers, but they are out

programme.title: Hushabye Lullabye
programme.desc: It's wash day for Dillie Dally! The Bath-time Ducks enjoy a swim in a bubbly bath and play hide and seek amongst the bubbles. Pop Pop! Pop! Can Dillie Dally find them?

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Captain America and The Avengers star Chris Evans reads Goodnight World, by Debi Gliori
