programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a Birmingham property with a tiny kitchen, a former care home near Conwy and a house in Merseyside

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The agents attempt to round up six horses that have been abandoned on an unsuspecting family's field. Ben and Myles meet another Sheriff as they arrive at a carpet showroom

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A doctor and a paramedic try to save the life of a hiker who fell 650ft, while a motorist takes evasive action when a car heads towards him on the wrong side of the road

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid makes a connection, while Daniel and Zara struggle as Al masterminds the last labour. Bear misses the treasure hunt to try to help a patient in need

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Charlotte, Dylan, Karen and Jay

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Philip and Lorraine in Stockport want to transform their forgotten plot into a stately garden, on a budget of £4,750. Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich Brothers to win the pitch

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: A trip through Scotland sees Natasha Raskin Sharp get to grips with haggis, while Steven Moore falls for an exquisite French lady

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway veers off the coastal path to walk along the Mawddach trail near Barmouth, taking an early-morning canoe trip and hearing a story of incredible bravery. He also visits the village of Fairbourne, and takes a ride on its special small-scale train. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones goes in search of local gold and Matt Bassett goes stargazing, but is defeated by the Welsh weather

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben meets up with Callum only to find him with a colleague and quickly senses he is not welcome. Kheerat opens up to Sharon about Chantelle. Phil and Kat take Bert and Ernie to the school event, where they bump into Jack and Raymond. However, on the way home Kat finds herself in a frightening situation

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Aisling Bea is guest host this time around, with comedian Roisin Conaty and journalist Clive Myrie joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: A special regal edition sees a member of the royal family welcomed to the studio. But can Alan maintain his dignity in the face of a career-defining setback? Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: Lockdown is finally lifted, and in Bridgend Scott gets to open his doughnuts and coffee cafe. Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos auditions Russian cook Shev for the role of head chef, while vintage clothing entrepreneur and keyboard player Nanial prepares for his band to launch their first album

programme.title: Gary Barlow: I'm with the Band
programme.desc: The Take That star brings together artists and musicians to perform classic songs from past and present. The band comprises Jamie Cullum (piano and vocals), James Bay (guitar and vocals), Mica Paris (vocals), Guy Chambers (keyboards and musical director), Tom Fletcher (guitar and vocals), Danya Fisher (bass) and Donavan Hepburn (drums), and of course Gary on keys and vocals. He also welcomes special guests to the show, as Anne-Marie and Craig David drop by and collaborate with Gary and the band, talk about their careers and musical heroes, and perform new versions of classic tracks

programme.title: A Fish Called Wanda (1988)
programme.desc: A stuffy English barrister representing a jewel thief is targeted by his client's partners in crime - a seductive American crook and her dim-witted would-be philosopher lover who will go to any lengths to discover where the diamonds from their last heist have been hidden. Crime comedy, starring John Cleese, Jamie Lee Curtis, Kevin Kline, Michael Palin and Tom Georgeson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Theo Randall, Ching-He Huang and Oti Mabuse. Olly Smith joins via video link and selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain concludes her culinary road trip by sampling what Wales has to offer. She heads to the Brecon Beacons National Park to meet sheep farmer Richard Roderick, who gives her a crash course in the essential skills of a shepherdess. In return, she cooks him lamb bhuna, served with garlic naan. In Monmouth, Nadiya visits Desdemona Freeman and Denise Jones, the UK's only homegrown edible rosewater makers, harvesting petals and creating a rose and raspberry fool

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a detached house in Colchester and a one-bedroom flat in Bellshill, North Lanarkshire, and Dion Dublin looks at a mid-terrace property in Croydon

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss assist two teams at an antiques fair in Oswestry, Shropshire, while presenter Eric Knowles hears about an artwork made out of confiscated knife blades

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Hull FC v St Helens (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the opening semi-final, which comes from Leigh Sports Village. The Airlie Birds are looking to move a step closer to emulating their 2016 and 2017 triumphs in this competition, but face a tough test against the reigning Super League champions, who surprisingly have not lifted this trophy since 2008. With commentary from Dave Woods

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: Snowdonia 1890
programme.desc: The Jones and Braddock families prepare to leave the 1890s smallholding and return to their 21st-century lives, but face a visit from the landlord's agent and a final reckoning at the quarry. Narrated by Matthew Rhys

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are TV presenter Radzi Chinyanganya, actress Ruth Madeley, politician Ann Widdecombe and TV personality Tom Read Wilson, who answer questions on Olympic sprinters, Will and Grace, The Escape of King Charles II and My Fair Lady

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a special celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, as a host of famous faces compete to win £10,000 for a charity of their choice. The contestants are Strictly Come Dancing professional dancers AJ Pritchard and his brother Curtis, journalist and newsreader Kate Silverton, TV presenter Anneka Rice, resident This Morning doctor Dr Ranj Singh, and Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A mother and daughter from Buckinghamshire answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win a life-changing sum they plan to put towards their love of animals. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman introduce a line-up of fellow TV presenters as contestants. Matthew Wright, Rav Wilding, Paul Martin, Holly Hamilton, Mark Lane, Janet Street-Porter, Martin Roberts and Fee Mak take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lev finally feels able to be open about his sexuality and his relationship with Xander, but a homophobic chance encounter leads him to destroy his own chance of happiness. Ethan is forced to consider the future of his family after he loses control of his own hand during surgery, and Charlie receives an unexpected visitor who claims to know him

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob admits that he does not feel ready to move in with Tina, so she responds to the rejection by flirting with Matthew in front of him in an attempt to make him jealous. Lev is facing an assault charge and hiding out at a hotel to avoid telling the kids the truth about himself. Faith turns to Xander for help in reaching out to him. Robyn is startled to find her old school friend Paul has started as the new receptionist temp in the ED

programme.title: Cristiano Ronaldo: Impossible to Ignore
programme.desc: The story of Portuguese football star Cristiano Ronaldo's life and career, revealing how he became the world's biggest sporting brand

programme.title: Three Days That Shook Football
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes of a chaotic three days in European football, as a proposed Super League was announced and then collapsed when faced with a wall of opposition

programme.title: The Hurt Locker (2008)
programme.desc: Two American bomb disposal experts operating in Iraq are assigned a new leader after their previous one dies in action. However, the sergeant's reckless attitude and love of battle consistently puts them at risk. Kathryn Bigelow's Oscar-winning drama, starring Jeremy Renner, Anthony Mackie, Guy Pearce and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Waled, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Pilgrims Les Dennis, Lesley Joseph, Brendan Cole, Stephen K Amos, Katy Brand, Greg Rutherford, Dana and Mehreen Baig visit Bagno Vignoni - well known for its hot springs - where Greg and Brendan take the plunge in the public pool. Meanwhile, at the top of the hillside, Dana, Stephen, Les and Katy visit the Roman bath formerly used by Popes, and explore the history of St Catherine of Siena, who also used to stay here. The group members travel on to the medieval city of Viterbo, from where they will have to walk every step of the final 100km to Rome. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, where the red and blue teams explore an antique centre in the company of experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian. The auction of their gatherings is later held in nearby Chiswick

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins visits Salisbury Cathedral to celebrate 30 years of the girls' choir, and Laura Wright meets Christian musicians at Coventry's Nexus Institute of Creative Arts

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a mother and daughter find a country home in Norfolk with a budget of £335,000, and follows a paper trail to uncover an endangered decorative heritage craft

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: The golden eagle became extinct in Wales 400 years ago, but an escaped bird has been living in the Cambrian Mountains for 10 years. Iolo Williams scours woodlands and hills in an attempt to find this magnificent bird of prey

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway veers off the coastal path to walk along the Mawddach trail near Barmouth, taking an early-morning canoe trip and hearing a story of incredible bravery. He also visits the village of Fairbourne, and takes a ride on its special small-scale train. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones goes in search of local gold and Matt Bassett goes stargazing, but is defeated by the Welsh weather

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of British and international TV programmes and talent, with the programme coming from the BBC's Television Centre in White City, west London. The Crown, I Hate Suzie, Gangs of London and Save Me Too are up for Best Drama Series, while other nominees include John Boyega, Josh O'Connor, Daisy Edgar-Jones, Graham Norton and Ant & Dec and the show features performances by Years & Years and Alexis Ffrench

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Prison drama, starring Sean Bean and Stephen Graham. A newly arrived prisoner has to adapt quickly to his dangerous new surroundings, while reflecting on the crime that brought him here. He finds himself sharing a cell with a troubled young man who murdered his father and is suffering panic attacks. Meanwhile, an officer encounters a threat against his son, who is serving time in a different prison

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from recent series, including Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness attempting to drive Insurance write offs around a terrifying Wall of Death. Plus, Chris reviews the Ferrari SF90 and the boys race around Alton Towers in electric cars

programme.title: Hayley Goes
programme.desc: To jab or not to jab? That's the question Hayley Pearce sets out to answer as she tries to understand the fears and confusion surrounding the Covid-19 vaccine. On the way she attends an anti-mask and vaccination demo in London, does a shift at a Covid testing centre and meets frontline medical staff and scientists to separate fact from social media-driven fiction

The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to upcycler Jacqui Joseph to transform a pair of chairs and a laundry basket, while designer-maker Daniel Heath hopes to give a small melamine dining table a stylish update. A pair of French armoires give Sarah herself an exciting new challenge

programme.title: Casualty

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of British and international TV programmes and talent, with the programme coming from the BBC's Television Centre in White City, west London. The Crown, I Hate Suzie, Gangs of London and Save Me Too are up for Best Drama Series, while other nominees include John Boyega, Josh O'Connor, Daisy Edgar-Jones, Graham Norton and Ant & Dec and the show features performances by Years & Years and Alexis Ffrench

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Prison drama, starring Sean Bean and Stephen Graham. A newly arrived prisoner has to adapt quickly to his dangerous new surroundings, while reflecting on the crime that brought him here. He finds himself sharing a cell with a troubled young man who murdered his father and is suffering panic attacks. Meanwhile, an officer encounters a threat against his son, who is serving time in a different prison

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Luxembourg v Scotland. Action from the friendly at Stade Josy Barthel, as Scotland play their final match before Euro 2020

programme.title: Wild Weekends
programme.desc: BBC Radio 1 DJ Jeremiah Asiamah pushes three complete strangers outside of their comfort zones on a voyage of self-discovery in Torridon in the Highlands. Jeremiah's friend and outdoorsman Zeki Basan shows them how to set up camp under the stars, offers alternative cooking methods and helps them conquer an epic climb of more than 2,000ft

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: Amy Irons and David Farrell chat to Line of Duty star Owen Teale, who is currently filming The Rig with Martin Compston. James Harkness talks to Jodie McCallum about his role in Channel 5's Anne Boleyn biopic

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from recent series, including Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness attempting to drive Insurance write offs around a terrifying Wall of Death. Plus, Chris reviews the Ferrari SF90 and the boys race around Alton Towers in electric cars

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a Birmingham property with a tiny kitchen, a former care home near Conwy and a house in Merseyside

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The agents attempt to round up six horses that have been abandoned on an unsuspecting family's field. Ben and Myles meet another Sheriff as they arrive at a carpet showroom

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A doctor and a paramedic try to save the life of a hiker who fell 650ft, while a motorist takes evasive action when a car heads towards him on the wrong side of the road

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid makes a connection, while Daniel and Zara struggle as Al masterminds the last labour. Bear misses the treasure hunt to try to help a patient in need

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Charlotte, Dylan, Karen and Jay

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a property in Oxfordshire on a budget of £900,000, and finds out about charcoal-making the environmentally friendly way

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Philip and Lorraine in Stockport want to transform their forgotten plot into a stately garden, on a budget of £4,750. Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich Brothers to win the pitch

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: A trip through Scotland sees Natasha Raskin Sharp get to grips with haggis, while Steven Moore falls for an exquisite French lady

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben meets up with Callum only to find him with a colleague and quickly senses he is not welcome. Kheerat opens up to Sharon about Chantelle. Phil and Kat take Bert and Ernie to the school event, where they bump into Jack and Raymond. However, on the way home Kat finds herself in a frightening situation

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Aisling Bea is guest host this time around, with comedian Roisin Conaty and journalist Clive Myrie joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: A special regal edition sees a member of the royal family welcomed to the studio. But can Alan maintain his dignity in the face of a career-defining setback? Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Gary Barlow: I'm with the Band
programme.desc: The Take That star brings together artists and musicians to perform classic songs from past and present. The band comprises Jamie Cullum (piano and vocals), James Bay (guitar and vocals), Mica Paris (vocals), Guy Chambers (keyboards and musical director), Tom Fletcher (guitar and vocals), Danya Fisher (bass) and Donavan Hepburn (drums), and of course Gary on keys and vocals. He also welcomes special guests to the show, as Anne-Marie and Craig David drop by and collaborate with Gary and the band, talk about their careers and musical heroes, and perform new versions of classic tracks

programme.title: A Fish Called Wanda (1988)
programme.desc: A stuffy English barrister representing a jewel thief is targeted by his client's partners in crime - a seductive American crook and her dim-witted would-be philosopher lover who will go to any lengths to discover where the diamonds from their last heist have been hidden. Crime comedy, starring John Cleese, Jamie Lee Curtis, Kevin Kline, Michael Palin and Tom Georgeson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Theo Randall, Ching-He Huang and Oti Mabuse. Olly Smith joins via video link and selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain concludes her culinary road trip by sampling what Wales has to offer. She heads to the Brecon Beacons National Park to meet sheep farmer Richard Roderick, who gives her a crash course in the essential skills of a shepherdess. In return, she cooks him lamb bhuna, served with garlic naan. In Monmouth, Nadiya visits Desdemona Freeman and Denise Jones, the UK's only homegrown edible rosewater makers, harvesting petals and creating a rose and raspberry fool

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a detached house in Colchester and a one-bedroom flat in Bellshill, North Lanarkshire, and Dion Dublin looks at a mid-terrace property in Croydon

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss assist two teams at an antiques fair in Oswestry, Shropshire, while presenter Eric Knowles hears about an artwork made out of confiscated knife blades

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Hull FC v St Helens (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the opening semi-final, which comes from Leigh Sports Village. The Airlie Birds are looking to move a step closer to emulating their 2016 and 2017 triumphs in this competition, but face a tough test against the reigning Super League champions, who surprisingly have not lifted this trophy since 2008. With commentary from Dave Woods

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to upcycler Jacqui Joseph to transform a pair of chairs and a laundry basket, while designer-maker Daniel Heath hopes to give a small melamine dining table a stylish update. A pair of French armoires give Sarah herself an exciting new challenge

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are TV presenter Radzi Chinyanganya, actress Ruth Madeley, politician Ann Widdecombe and TV personality Tom Read Wilson, who answer questions on Olympic sprinters, Will and Grace, The Escape of King Charles II and My Fair Lady

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a special celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, as a host of famous faces compete to win £10,000 for a charity of their choice. programme.desc: Pilgrims Les Dennis, Lesley Joseph, Brendan Cole, Stephen K Amos, Katy Brand, Greg Rutherford, Dana and Mehreen Baig visit Bagno Vignoni - well known for its hot springs - where Greg and Brendan take the plunge in the public pool. Meanwhile, at the top of the hillside, Dana, Stephen, Les and Katy visit the Roman bath formerly used by Popes, and explore the history of St Catherine of Siena, who also used to stay here. programme.desc: Nya uses her elemental powers to coerce the Munces into agreeing to a truce meeting with the Geckles - at the same time, Kai and Zane are forced to resort to trickery in the Geckle camp in order to manipulate the Geckles to attend the truce meeting. The Skull Sorcerer learns of the meeting and uses the skull of Hazza D'Ur to resurrect the fearsome dragon Grief-Bringer!

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Loose Women panellist Brenda Edwards and actress Charlotte Ritchie join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by comedian Ed Byrne and there are recipes for ribs, sole and pork chops, while guest chefs Tom Kerridge, Angela Hartnett and Daniel Clifford also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode prepares jerk chicken with rice and peas, while Lisa Faulkner makes a vegetarian curry full of paneer and potatoes

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of L'Isle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani are at Epsom for Derby Day - the biggest day of racing in the flat season. Today's feature race is the title event of the week - the Cazoo Derby at 4.30 - in which a select field of three-year-old horses bid for a place in immortality by passing the famous Epsom winning post in first place. There are four competitive supporting races including the Dash - the fastest five-furlong race anywhere in the world. With analysis from Jason Weaver, Hayley Turner and Johnny Murtagh, reporting from Rishi Persad, Mick Fitzgerald and Matt Chapman, and social stable news with Oli Bell, Chris Hughes and Leonna Mayor, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Anton Du Beke, Alistair McGowan, Sarah-Jane Crawford and Adrian Edmondson pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Torquay for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: The Masked Dancer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts as the contest reaches its conclusion, with Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Mo Gilligan and Oti Mabuse joined by guest panellist Holly Willoughby. All 12 celebrities return to give a spectacular opening routine, then the four finalists each perform twice before the identities of all of them are revealed and one is crowned the winner

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams narrates another compilation of TV cock-ups, all rescued from the cutting-room floor to delight the nation. Holly Willoughby reveals too much to Russell Crowe, Rylan Clark-Neal comes unstuck on the set of Supermarket Sweep, Kourtney Kardashian causes trouble on Australian news, and James Martin finds live TV is a recipe for disaster

programme.title: Casino Royale (1967)
programme.desc: Ageing spy James Bond comes out of retirement to tackle a mysterious mastermind who wants to take over the world. Meanwhile, another agent disguises himself as Bond to face the evil genius over the baccarat table. Spoof spy adventure, starring David Niven, Peter Sellers, Woody Allen, Orson Welles, Deborah Kerr and Ursula Andress

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Torquay for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality (2000)
programme.desc: A feminist FBI agent is sent undercover as a contestant in the Miss United States pageant when the event becomes the target of a terrorist bomb threat. As she is not exactly catwalk material, a veteran British coach is brought on board to oversee her transformation into a glamorous beauty queen. Comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Michael Caine, Benjamin Bratt, Candice Bergen and William Shatner

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Romania (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of England's final Euro 2020 warm-up game, which takes place at Riverside Stadium in Middlesbrough. With just a week to go until their European Championship campaign begins against Croatia at Wembley, Gareth Southgate gets one last chance here to assess his squad and fine-tune his tactics. Romania failed to qualify for the tournament this time around, although their national stadium in Bucharest is hosting three group-stage matches along with one last-16 fixture. With analysis from Ian Wright, Roy Keane and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: It's five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mother's past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Romania and Luxembourg v Scotland. Mark Pougatch introduces action from the Euro 2020 warm-up friendlies, which took place at Riverside Stadium and Stade Josy Barthel. Plus, highlights of Austria v Slovakia, Netherlands v Georgia and Belgium v Croatia. Ian Wright and Lee Dixon provide expert analysis

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp takes his team of England legends across Europe to tackle the continent's soccer superpower Germany on their home soil. Joining Harry again as assistant manager is John Barnes, with the team including David Seaman, Paul Merson, Matt Le Tissier, Rob Lee, Ray Parlour, Lee Sharpe, Mark Chamberlain, Mark Wright, Razor Ruddock and Lee Hendrie

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: Olympic Boxing gold medallist and Strictly star Nicola Adams begins her adventure with Bear Grylls in a quarry in the heart of Dartmoor National Park. Her first challenge is to abseil down a 150-foot sheer cliff. Later, she needs to quickly master a technique where you use upper body strength to pull yourself up a rope

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A fisherman's body is found tangled up in a North Sea trawler's net. Vera and her team investigate with the help of the local harbourmaster, and find the dead man's sons pointing the finger at the owner of a fish market, with whom they have a long-running feud. Retracing the dead man's steps, Vera discovers when he drowned and tracks down a waitress who may have been one of the last people to see him alive. However, the case becomes more complicated when a witness runs away and a boat belonging to the dead man's sister-in-law goes up in flames

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of a young man's body in an area notorious for illicit teenage gatherings, the corpse wrapped in plastic sheeting and having received a blow to the head. Vera follows the trail to a funfair where the victim used to work and a church where he sought refuge, having recently been made homeless. She delves into his family history to discover why he was on the streets when his parents lived nearby, while forensic evidence implicates the son of the vicar, who has a criminal past

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when the body of a wildlife ranger is found on a remote island off the coast of Northumberland. programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old pal of Greengrass's turns up with a money-making scheme, but the pair are soon out of their depth and Nick is forced to go undercover to sort it out. Meanwhile, Ventress tries to organise a marquee for his daughter's wedding - with disappointing results. Police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Angie warns Sean to expect a visit from CID about Chris's disappearance. Zoe is convinced Chris is dead. Elsa has some devastating news for Kathy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy and Laura discuss the impending custody case. Bernice's devotion to Feng Shui angers Alan. Zoe tells Laura she is going to buy Home Farm

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita worries about what is happening to her health. The Websters return from America. Spider is angry when Fred and Alec attempt to bribe him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when the body of a wildlife ranger is found on a remote island off the coast of Northumberland. Her death is at first assumed to be an accident, since she was alone on the island when she died, but marks on the body seem to point to murder. As Vera delves into the dead woman's personal life, she uncovers dark secrets and old resentments

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.desc: The day of the Cromer game dawns and the team are determined to beat Fatty Woollacott's side at all costs - but a mysterious incident in Dorking threatens to put a spanner in the works. Comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: The Queen's Coronation in Colour
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong looks back on the coronation for the 65th anniversary, and introduces some of the best quality colour footage of the event that survives to this day. Trevor McDonald, Len Goodman, Michael Crawford and Alison Steadman share their memories of the festivities, while royal insiders including Prince Michael of Kent recall being directly involved in the historic moment

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is discovered on the Northumberland moors, and identified as a woman who made a distressed 999 call on the night of her death - suggesting she may have been abducted by someone she knew. Vera takes on the case, and discovers the victim was a controversial figure in her home town, who had a complicated relationship with her family and a number of well-hidden secrets

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Tensions between Tom and his labourers erupt during the Sanditon cricket match. Charlotte and Sidney continue to clash, resulting in Miss Lambe being placed in terrible danger, while Lord Babington offers Esther a glimpse of a better future. Period drama, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Kris Marshall, Crystal Clarke and Charlotte Spencer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A fisherman's body is found tangled up in a North Sea trawler's net. Vera and her team investigate with the help of the local harbourmaster, and find the dead man's sons pointing the finger at the owner of a fish market, with whom they have a long-running feud. Retracing the dead man's steps, Vera discovers when he drowned and tracks down a waitress who may have been one of the last people to see him alive. However, the case becomes more complicated when a witness runs away and a boat belonging to the dead man's sister-in-law goes up in flames

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of a young man's body in an area notorious for illicit teenage gatherings, the corpse wrapped in plastic sheeting and having received a blow to the head. Vera follows the trail to a funfair where the victim used to work and a church where he sought refuge, having recently been made homeless. She delves into his family history to discover why he was on the streets when his parents lived nearby, while forensic evidence implicates the son of the vicar, who has a criminal past

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when the body of a wildlife ranger is found on a remote island off the coast of Northumberland. programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day six of the second Grand Slam of the year, which comes from Roland Garros, where the men's and women's third-round matches are scheduled to get under way. Rafael Nadal continued his march to a 13th title here with a comfortable 6-1, 6-4, 6-0 win over Stefano Travaglia, after top women's seed Simona Halep had also been in dominant form as she swept aside Amanda Anisimova 6-0, 6-1. Elsewhere, there was a memorable victory for home wild card Hugo Gaston, as he overcame 2015 champion Stan Wawrinka in five sets. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage six, which featured a 168km course from Loriol-Sur-Drome to Le Sappey-en-Chartreuse. Gary Imlach presents, with commentary by Simon Brotherton and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a selection of interviews, clips and profiles from the ITV archives featuring some of the biggest names in sport. In the first edition, Muhammad Ali discusses his ban from boxing and the Rumble in the Jungle, and takes part in an impromptu sparring session, while Brian Clough has a heated on-screen debate with Don Revie, his predecessor at Leeds. Plus, a revealing portrait of Welsh rugby talisman Alun Wyn Jones

programme.title: When Football Changed Forever
programme.desc: The dramatic story of the 1991/92 Football League Division One season - the final campaign before the Premier League began. Leeds United overcame fierce rivals Manchester United to emerge as champions, thanks to a certain Frenchman named Eric Cantona

programme.title: The Hand of God
programme.desc: A look back at England v Argentina during the 1986 FIFA World Cup, famous for the late Diego Maradona scoring two goals in a 2-1 victory, which were notable for different reasons. Featuring contributions from members of the England team that played that day, including Peter Shilton, Glen Hoddle, Terry Butcher, Gary Lineker, Steve Hodge and Kenny Sansom

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell, Matt Chapman, Francesca Cumani, Jason Weaver and Rishi Persad are at Epsom on the morning of the 2021 Derby - the biggest race of the flat season

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the seventh day's play at Roland Garros, where the third-round singles matches are scheduled to conclude in the Grand Slam tournament. Top seed Novak Djokovic continued his march to the final with a routine 6-3, 6-3, 6-2 victory over Daniel Elahi Galan at this stage last year, but seventh seed Matteo Berettini suffered a shock 6-2, 7-6, 6-4 defeat at the hands of qualifier Daniel Altmaier. In the women's competition, home player Fiona Ferro prevailed in three sets against Patricia Matia Tig and two-time Wimbledon champion Petra Kvitova won 7-5, 6-3 against Leylah Annie Fernandez. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage seven, featuring a 171km course from Saint-Martin-le-Vinoux to La Plagne. Gary Imlach presents, with commentary by Simon Brotherton and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: Predators (2010)
programme.desc: Hardened fighters from all over the world, including military veterans and ruthless criminals, are abducted by aliens and transported to a dangerous jungle world. As they struggle to survive, they discover why they have been brought to the planet - to be used as prey in a big-game hunt. Sci-fi thriller sequel, with Adrien Brody, Topher Grace, Alice Braga, Danny Trejo and Laurence Fishburne

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: A mansion break-in targeting alcohol and other valuables turns into a murder inquiry after the owner's returns unexpectedly. Murtaugh and Riggs link a jacket found at the scene to Riana's friend Phoebe, and back at home Murtaugh and Trish question their trust in Riana as they wonder where her loyalties lie. Elsewhere, Riggs is delighted to see Ben joining Little League, recalling his troubling childhood and feeling he has found a true family with Molly and Ben. However, the youngster soon falls foul of his coach. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring fourth-round singles matches in the Grand Slam tournament. Eventual champion Iga Swiatek produced a dominant display at this stage last year to overwhelm top seed Simona Halep 6-1, 6-2, before men's champion Rafael Nadal overcame qualifier Sebastian Korda in an equally one-sided contest. Following those matches, the women's and men's number three seeds progressed in contrasting fashions against home players, with Elena Svitolina winning 6-1, 6-3 against Caroline Garcia, and Dominic Thiem taking five sets to defeat Hugo Caston. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cups

programme.title: Get Shirty
programme.desc: Documentary examining how a small underwear company from Leicester turned the world of football upside down when it invented a replica kit and established an industry now worth billions of pounds

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: The Yangtze - the longest river in Asia and the third-longest in the world - was once home to rare aquatic mammals and prodigious quantities of fish. Curious to know what still lurks in its murky currents, Jeremy explores Shanghai, the huge Three Gorges Dam and the wild uplands of Yunnan. The people he meets make him question his own preconceptions as a biologist and naturalist

In the first edition, Muhammad Ali discusses his ban from boxing and the Rumble in the Jungle, and takes part in an impromptu sparring session, while Brian Clough has a heated on-screen debate with Don Revie, his predecessor at Leeds. Plus, a revealing portrait of Welsh rugby talisman Alun Wyn Jones

programme.title: When Football Changed Forever
programme.desc: The dramatic story of the 1991/92 Football League Division One season - the final campaign before the Premier League began. Leeds United overcame fierce rivals Manchester United to emerge as champions, thanks to a certain Frenchman named Eric Cantona

programme.title: The Hand of God
programme.desc: A look back at England v Argentina during the 1986 FIFA World Cup, famous for the late Diego Maradona scoring two goals in a 2-1 victory, which were notable for different reasons. Featuring contributions from members of the England team that played that day, including Peter Shilton, Glen Hoddle, Terry Butcher, Gary Lineker, Steve Hodge and Kenny Sansom

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell, Matt Chapman, Francesca Cumani, Jason Weaver and Rishi Persad are at Epsom on the morning of the 2021 Derby - the biggest race of the flat season

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the seventh day's play at Roland Garros, where the third-round singles matches are scheduled to conclude in the Grand Slam tournament. Top seed Novak Djokovic continued his march to the final with a routine 6-3, 6-3, 6-2 victory over Daniel Elahi Galan at this stage last year, but seventh seed Matteo Berettini suffered a shock 6-2, 7-6, 6-4 defeat at the hands of qualifier Daniel Altmaier. In the women's competition, home player Fiona Ferro prevailed in three sets against Patricia Matia Tig and two-time Wimbledon champion Petra Kvitova won 7-5, 6-3 against Leylah Annie Fernandez. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage seven, featuring a 171km course from Saint-Martin-le-Vinoux to La Plagne. Gary Imlach presents, with commentary by Simon Brotherton and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: Predators (2010)
programme.desc: Hardened fighters from all over the world, including military veterans and ruthless criminals, are abducted by aliens and transported to a dangerous jungle world. As they struggle to survive, they discover why they have been brought to the planet - to be used as prey in a big-game hunt. Sci-fi thriller sequel, with Adrien Brody, Topher Grace, Alice Braga, Danny Trejo and Laurence Fishburne

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: A mansion break-in targeting alcohol and other valuables turns into a murder inquiry after the owner's returns unexpectedly. Murtaugh and Riggs link a jacket found at the scene to Riana's friend Phoebe, and back at home Murtaugh and Trish question their trust in Riana as they wonder where her loyalties lie. Elsewhere, Riggs is delighted to see Ben joining Little League, recalling his troubling childhood and feeling he has found a true family with Molly and Ben. However, the youngster soon falls foul of his coach. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring fourth-round singles matches in the Grand Slam tournament. Eventual champion Iga Swiatek produced a dominant display at this stage last year to overwhelm top seed Simona Halep 6-1, 6-2, before men's champion Rafael Nadal overcame qualifier Sebastian Korda in an equally one-sided contest. Following those matches, the women's and men's number three seeds progressed in contrasting fashions against home players, with Elena Svitolina winning 6-1, 6-3 against Caroline Garcia, and Dominic Thiem taking five sets to defeat Hugo Caston. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cups
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a rundown of the best 50 goals from the FIFA World Cup, featuring memorable strikes from Michael Owen, Dennis Bergkamp, Diego Maradona and Pele among the contenders for the most unforgettable World Cup goal in the history of the tournament

programme.title: Get Shirty
programme.desc: Documentary examining how a small underwear company from Leicester turned the world of football upside down when it invented a replica kit and established an industry now worth billions of pounds

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: The Yangtze - the longest river in Asia and the third-longest in the world - was once home to rare aquatic mammals and prodigious quantities of fish. Curious to know what still lurks in its murky currents, Jeremy explores Shanghai, the huge Three Gorges Dam and the wild uplands of Yunnan. The people he meets make him question his own preconceptions as a biologist and naturalist

programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Gran has been particularly naughty lately and is sentenced to a stay in an old people's home, but the home is the stuff of nightmares for Gran, and it's soon up to the Spuds to break her free.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave and his friends get lost on a school orienteering exercise thanks to Robert Robot, who is unable to read the map - snatched by a flock of giant tiger pigeons and deposited into their equally giant nest, Dave must retrieve the map from Robert and get them all back to school.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave stumbles across a mermaid at the dockside who invites herself round for tea and takes up residence in the Spuds' bathtub, so Dave and his family must somehow persuade their unwanted guest to go back to her watery home.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave and his friends embark on a school trip along a canal where they awaken an ancient monster called Big Lippy, who pushes them over a waterfall and sends them back in time to the Jurassic era.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave finds a handheld computer game at the dump which involves killing zombies - his friend Zombie Katie, however, finds the game rather insulting. Exacting her revenge, Dave finds himself confronted with a group of very angry and very real zombies.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is warned by local fortune teller Madam Donald that walking under a ladder will bring bad luck - he walks under one anyway, and soon a meteor is heading for Grimsby. It is up to Dave and his friend Robert Robot to catch it before everyone is squished.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Anna is training to be a magician with Dave as her helpful assistant, but their disappearing trick is not up to much - that is, until Zombie Katie bestows a magic spell book upon them. Soon they can't stop making stuff disappear, but what will happen when there is nothing left to make disappear in Grimsby?

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Lil Sue has started a delivery company called Deliver Sue, and in a quest to raise money for a new set of wheels, Dave lands himself with a job - Anna thinks she knows how to deliver packages quicker than Dave, though, and a dramatic sibling rivalry soon unfolds.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is thrilled when he wins an electronic home help device called Barry - finally, his family can boss something around other than him, but it is not long before Barry is overwhelmed by the Spuds' demands. Fighting back, Barry takes over Grimsby Heights and heads off to the sea for some well-earned peace and quiet.

programme.desc: In the city that never sleeps, the documentary discovers a homelessness epidemic in New York's shelter system, made worse by the Covid crisis. With one epidemic feeding another, there is now more homelessness in New York than at any time since the Great Depression, and it's disproportionately affecting black and brown people

programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets energy conservationist Andrew, who has decided to build a radical, self-heating home for his family - the first of its kind in the UK. He aims to heat the house all year round by storing the warmth of the summer sun into insulated earth banks over and around the house, and then drawing the warmth back into the living spaces in winter. However, this pioneering construction seems an exercise in trial and error from the start, and what began as a seemingly simple, pure concept becomes a complicated feat of engineering

programme.title: Gregg Wallace's Big Weekend Away
programme.desc: The presenter has always wanted to visit the German capital, and over three days he digs into Berlin's fascinating history and food. Gregg Wallace gets stuck into some local classics - including currywurst, pretzels and steins of beer - but also discovers dishes distinct to this city that reveal an often overlooked part of Germany's food traditions

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Austria's stunning Transalpine railway, which crosses the snowy peaks of the Tirol. Our journey begins in the historic city of Graz and heads west through famous alpine beauty spots, including Zell am Zee, where the sounds of yodelling still echo across pristine mountain valleys. The final stretch passes through the 10.6- kilometre Arlberg Tunnel. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: Revisiting the musical highs of 1993 in the company of Cliff Richard, Gabrielle, Shaun Ryder, Adamski, Jayne Middlemiss, Paul Gambaccini, Mark Radcliffe and others. It was the time Annie Lennox, Spin Doctors and 4 Non Blondes dominated the charts, but what was the biggest selling single of the year?

programme.title: Diana Ross: Secrets of Her Biggest Hits

programme.title: Secret Scotland with Susan Calman
programme.desc: Susan heads behind the scenes at the famed Gretna Green, the UK's wedding capital, and uncovers the secrets of weddings past. She then follows the course of the world famous River Tweed, trying her hand at flyfishing as she goes, before meeting the weavers at the last commercial handwoven tartan mill in the world. It's there Susan discovers that her own ancestors would have needed quite a large wardrobe for all of the tartans to which the Calmans can lay claim. Finally, the comedian learns the secrets of sheep herding from a border collie

Later, they go to the village of Cookham, where they take in the Stanley Spencer Art Gallery, before finishing their travels in Henley-on-Thames, where they enjoy a roast dinner.

programme.title: The Queen Mother
programme.desc: A look at the final 50 years of the Queen Mother's life, which she dedicated to her family and the country, devoting herself to official duties, functions and openings, which would become her full-time occupation. programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow charts the life of Greek artist Domenicos Theotocopolous, dubbed El Greco, one of the most distinctive painters of the 16th century. His work was considered to be ahead of its time - so much so that it was all but ignored for 300 years. El Greco developed a highly individualistic style that eventually proved enormously influential, paving the way for the Baroque and providing the inspiration for 20th-century expressionism

programme.title: Marmite: Love It or Loathe It?
programme.desc: Documentary examining the science behind the divisive food spread renowned for its distinctive flavour, as well as looking into its origins and its role in fighting two world wars. Superfans have their say and there is an unlikely Marmite-based recipe for cake enthusiasts, a selection of adverts, and an experiment to whether a hater can be converted into a lover

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes at Lily O'Brien's factory in Co Kildare, and follows chocolatier Chris Zammit as he takes on one of the most intricate chocolate sculptures he has ever made for the launch of a new hotel. Plus, a look at the history of the Cadbury Creme Egg, which was launched in the 1960s as an Easter special and soon a whopping 500 million of them were rolling off the production line every year

programme.title: 10 Years Younger in 10 Days
programme.desc: Cherry Healey and her team transform the look of Suzanne, who is a shadow of her former self after numerous operations including a hysterectomy and the removal of a brain tumour. They also give a makeover to Alison, who was fun-loving and confident, but caring for her elderly father, being a single mum and a tough time in lockdown has left her losing sight of who she is

programme.title: Our Cilla: The One and Only
programme.desc: The story of Cilla Black, who rose from working-class roots in Liverpool to become one of the nation's most successful and best-loved singers, presenters and all-round entertainers. Using rare and never-before-seen footage and interviews with friends and colleagues, the programme reveals just what made Cilla the woman she was, and what she was really like behind the scenes. With contributions by Alex and Sue Tatham, the first-ever Blind Date couple to get married, as well as Michael Grade, Christopher Biggins, Gloria Hunniford and Andrew Lloyd-Webber.

programme.title: The Story of
programme.desc: Profile of the talk show host, exploring his youth in Birkenhead and formative years facing a challenging career as a social worker. The programme looks back at his rise to fame in the guise of drag act alter ego Lily Savage, before shedding his make-up to emerge as a beloved presenter in his own right, presenting a series of award-winning chat shows and documentaries

programme.desc: Christian and Mandy, the organisers of the annual Pride festival, are 4,000 euros short of the 18,000 required to stage the weekend-long party - and if that were not bad enough, the costumes are seven sizes too big. With two weeks left, can the duo save the day? Cameras also follow bar owner Cath as she prepares to host a hen party for 14 excited Northern Irish women and Leeds lad Peter, who publishes a free pocket guide to the Costa del Sol

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas presents a whistle-stop tour of awkward, embarrassing and cringeworthy holiday moments, as related by a selection of comedians and celebrities. Johnny's famous contributors skewer the traditional staycation, questioning the perks of rain-drenched British beaches, and whether anyone really likes going camping. Turning their attention further afield, the stars share some of their most memorable holiday tales, including transforming a hotel corridor into one long 'slip-and-slide', the suspicious discovery of a pair of chicken fillets in a swimming pool in Marbella, and a blush-inducing incident involving a naked paragliding session along a Spanish beachfront

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century artist Peter Paul Rubens, who enjoyed an active and eventful career. He was regarded as the chief exponent of the Baroque style, merging the grace of the Italian High Renaissance with the realism and landscapes of the northern tradition, and was perhaps best known for painting voluptuous female nudes within large-scale mythological allegories
